Alien.Description:
A world in deep in blacklight.
(Wear lots of sunscreen.)

The atmosphere contains gases that are only passable for ultraviolet rays.
While those are invisible to the human eye, from surfaces they can still be reflected back as 'light' - Resulting in bleak-yet-colourful landscapes.

AridStarter.Description:
Parched mountains, rugged savanna and grueling plains, shriveled lake-beds with the remains of once thriving plant and animal life. This hot, dry and unrelenting planet has a thin atmosphere that is un-breathable by humans. A [c][FFFFFF]more challenging[-][/c] start for players that like to face the dangers of heat and the lack of oxygen.

[i]- Cool down with water; generate oxygen with the condenser.
- Hostile weather and aggressive neighbours will arrive. Be prepared.[/i]

Barren.Description: A planet with large amounts of fissile elements deep in its mantle, causing runaway nuclear reactions.

BarrenCooled.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

This barren rock carries its history on the surface: 
Completely solidified, and no atmosphere to remove tracks and shapes - 
All encounters will be remembered.

BarrenMetal.Description:
[c][e0e020]NOTE:[-][/c]   [c]Multiple anti-aircraft turrets detected. [-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Use extreme caution.[-][/i][-][/c]

During a planet's formation, most of the heavy elements sink down into the core.
But if a cosmic accident strips a world of its outer layers, or if there was abundant material - then there can be such a thing as a 'metal planet'.

In any case, there will be a 'thin' silicate mantle left, of 'just a few hundred' kilometers.
Still, there are vast quantities left to mine, and deposits of heavy and exotic elements are readily accessible, too.

Since this is a rare place, it attracts many 'protectors'. Only the really careful or the most powerful will find a way to navigate... -Trespassers, beware.

BarrenTemperate.Description:
[c][e0e020]Note:[-][/c][c] Risk of [c][e0e020][b]hostile patrols[/b][-][/c] ![-][/c]

[c]Resource scanner reports ERROR:
[b]"Cannot pinpoint deposit locations!"[/b]
[i]Densities are beyond calibration ranges.
Found: [b]Ti[/b], [b]Al[/b], Cu, Au, (Pt) ... ...   [c][808080]-Use visual clues[-][/c][/i]


This would be Earth, if it never would have experienced the wonders of self-replicating chemistry.
A planet-sized, lonely sandbox, a bachelor of stone.

Even if planting some seeds here, ... they would have a hard time to gain a foothold.
For complex life to flourish, it needs all the simpler stages to prepare the ground beforehand. Bacteria for plants, plants for animals...

So for now, geology rules, under a toxic sky.

But your visit might help to change that.
- Your whole body, your suit, your equipment... every surface inside and out... is an ark.

Those early microorganisms you carry with you could prepare the soil and air - - for new garden to flourish.

Just come back in half a billion years, and check it out!

CarbonPlateau.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

[c][e0e020]Note:[-][/c][c] Risk of [c][e0e020][b]hostile patrols[/b][-][/c] ![-][/c]

[b][i]Oceans of oil.[/b][/i]   *
[b][i]Diamond rain.[/b][/i]   **

Planets get sorted through by gravity, during their formation.
Heavy and dense materials sink to the inner. 
Low density substances rise to the surface, as a kind of 'slag'.

The usual result is: A metal core, and a mantle / crust out of Silicon-based rock.

But chemcially, Carbon is very similar to Silicon.
... It just might need a kind of very ... 'dirty' solar system, where this stuff is in abundance, to add an extra layer on top of the crust.

With this 'Carbon-based rock', much changes. Shape of mountains, continents...

____

[i](* Derivates)
(** Low-grade, unsellable, and [b]sharp[/b]) [/i]

Desert.Description:
Water is quite common in the universe, as life-enabling liquid, or at least as ice or vapor.
However, not every planet is gifted with water. Some worlds get scorched by their home star, or maybe their magnetic field is too weak to stem against the push of the stellar wind.

But even this place of glaring sunshine has managed to keep some spots here or there...

DesertBurnt.Description:
During its formation, this world never got much 'stirred through'.

Now it suffers from a stagnant core, and vast thermal differences between all rock layers - And no plate tectonics to release the pressure.

By that, the mantle layer cools far too quickly in comparison to the rest of the planet - Shrinking and cracking its surface like a giant egg shell.

And while there are usually no volcanoes, ... IF they do break through such a fissure, they tend to be ultra violent. - - With continent-sized lava flows and planet-wide ash covers.

Of such a recent eruption, there are still survivors:
Out of the drains and the burrows, life slowly explores a strange new world...

Ellyon_BarrenMetal.Description:
[c][e0e020]NOTE:[-][/c]   [c]Multiple anti-aircraft turrets detected. [-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Use extreme caution.[-][/i][-][/c]

During a planet's formation, most of the heavy elements sink down into the core.
But if a cosmic accident strips a world of its outer layers, or if there was abundant material - then there can be such a thing as a 'metal planet'.

In any case, there will be a 'thin' silicate mantle left, of 'just a few hundred' kilometers.
Still, there are vast quantities left to mine, and deposits of heavy and exotic elements are readily accessible, too.

Since this is a rare place, it attracts many 'protectors'. Only the really careful or the most powerful will find a way to navigate... -Trespassers, beware.

GS_Akua.Description:
Difficulty: Easy
Tutorial: YES
Mission: Clear Skies (Talon)

A charming, green paradise that will invite you in. But beware: Even the most harmless creatures will know how to defend their turfs and treasures!

GS_Ashon2.Description:
Ashon, a planet that had to undergo the same catastrophe that once befell the Proto-Earth and Theia.
It has just barely pulled itself together... What is left of the impactor is circling in the orbit - - Slowly forming another moon.

- Detecting pockets of surface lifeforms, yet no biosphere to sustain them. - Which implies an artificial origin?
The Zirax bases may be tasked to study those creatures - - Or, yet more likely, to kill them off.

GS_Ashon2Moon.Description:
A world in its youth: The atmosphere has cooled enough for it to rain. Last bits of the planetary neighbourhood crash on the surface, and violent jetstreams produce hail.

But in all this chaos, first life develops in the tide pools - filling the water with the colour of early photosynthesis.

GS_Beech.Description: An ocean world with only a few landmasses

GS_ConflictArid.Description:
[c][b]Veteran player survival start.[/b] [Seed-randomized. Hard]

[c][e0e020][b]Warning:[-][/c][/b]   [c]Several fighting, [b]hostile Patrol Vessels[/b] detected.
[c][e0e020][b]Warning:[-][/c][/b]   [c]Aggressive wildlife, No crash site protection. [-][/c]
[c][e0e020][b]Warning:[-][/c][/b]   [c]Toxic, sometimes radiated atmosphere.[-][/c]

[-][/c][i]This unforgiving world has turned into a conflict zone for several large alien factions...
You have no choice but to crash land right in between.[/i]

GS_Masperon.Description:
Difficulty: Medium
Tutorial: No
Mission: Bad Wolf (Zirax House of Abyssal)

This world grows more teeth with every heartbeat. If you survive and have no problem with lots of hungry mouths looking your way, find the old Zirax base. Your experience could be in demand...

GS_Ningues.Description:
Difficulty: Medium
Tutorial: NO
Mission: Andromeda Strain (Polaris Megacorp)

If you survive the first few hours on this ice-covered moon, find the Science Station and help the staff there with their challenges....

GS_Skillon.Description:
Difficulty: Medium to High
Tutorial: NO
Mission: Deserted Powers (Pirate Clans)

If you survive the first few hours on this radioactively contaminated former weapons test planet, look for the Vren clan's La Grande Maira. Maybe they need your help there! Or someone else needs it? You'll see...

HKT_Hestilion.Description:
A life-bearing world. Protected from radiation by a thick atmosphere - which also keeps the heat in.
Rich in water but poor in soils, which creates a fertile breeding ground for dangerous creatures.

HKT_Kantarr.Description:
Planets are born by the swirling, scattered ashes - the legacy of dead, exploded stars. 
...Gravity will collect all this messy debris, and give it a new form.

But a world's amount of rock and dust, slowly grinding, clumping together... will result in an enormous amount of friction.

Which means, [b][i]heat[/i][/b].

This place is just a mere quarter billion years old, and struggles to radiate all this excess heat into space -
The bigger the planet, the longer it takes.

In this liquid state, the planet resembles more a giant cooking pot, or lava lamp, or a blast furnace:
Continents of less dense slag skim the surface, while the heavy, exotic metals sink into the planet's deep inner.

Yet, by thermal churning and stirring, some bits of that precious core will be again swept back out, in reach of the surface.

For those who brave these temperatures, there will be great rewards.

HKT_Toenish.Description:
An older planet. Bright sun and dry land. 
With Plants and Fungi. 
[i]One[/i] of them invaded this world.
But after all this time, who can still tell...? 

The extremes died off, competition gave way to truce - And then to mutual exchange of nutrients. 
Until, suddenly, one life depends on the other.

HomeworldArkenian.Description:
The Arkenian Homeworld.

This planet lost nearly all of its water due to ancient deep-core bombardment.

HomeworldArkenian_Inst_Planet.Description:
The Arkenian Homeworld. 
 
This planet lost nearly all of its water due to ancient deep-core bombardement.

HomeworldHelios.Description:
Water is the great equalizer.

Is it too hot? Then it will soak up the energy, to keep it for later. - Still liquid!
Or too cold? - The water will move and spread the warmth around.

And its ice will swim, and insulate the ocean below from freezing solid.
By this, even a world of all-encompassing snow might change, evolve and thrive.

Dare to exist near to a fickle, unpredictable star? 
Or far away, at the outer edges of its generosity?
... Between hot and cold, with [i]water[/i] there is always a place for life!

HomeworldHeliosMoon.Description: A barren moon orbiting the Helios homeworld.

HomeworldKaranaMoon.Description:
A unique moon of unimaginable origins. Most planets have crystals of various kinds, but this is a truly unique marvel of the galaxy.

Crystal is not only the dominant feature of the moon, but its entire being. Whether the moon was terraformed is unknown, whether it's a random occurrence of astronomical odds is unknown.

What is known is the complete and utter rarity of the crystals this moon provides.

HomeworldKriel.Description:
The devastation on this planet is visible from far away, and what's more, the cause was not a natural event. 

If there was ever an atmosphere, then it's been entirely stripped away. There is no sign of life, even at the microbiological level, and in essence, this planet is the very definition of a lifeless rock endlessly circling its star.

Someone bombarded the planet from space, but who, what, why, and when are yet to be determined...

HomeworldLegacy.Description:
[i]Through your comms blares an automated message, half-garbled, with a timecode maybe hundreds of years old.[/i]

''... [c]Repeat. 
[c][e0e020][b][DANGER][/b]   Containment Zone[-][/c]
[c][b][i]First class[/b] - [b]unlimited[/b] - [b]eternal[/b] - [-][/c][c][e0e020][b][Bio][Rad][Psy][-][/c][/i][/b]

[c][i]By order of the Zirax High Command and the Surviving War Factions: 
Any vessel entering Legacy territory will be regarded as compromised and [b]destroyed on sight[/b].[/i]

[c][e0e020][b]THIS IS YOUR ONLY WARNING.[/b][-][/c]

[c]Repeat. 
[c][e0e020][DANGER]   Containment Zone [-][/c]...''

[i]Your ship is being scanned from this planet.[/i]

HomeworldPirates.Description: The home system of the pirates. Watch your purse.

HomeworldPrenn.Description: The homeworld of the Prenn Federation and the Traders Guild

HomeworldRavagersMoon.Description: The moon orbiting the Ravagers homeworld

HomeworldTresari.Description:
An unsettling world that's both toxic and dangerous.

The gases within the atmosphere shouldn't support life, yet here it thrives nonetheless.

HomeworldTresariMoon1.Description: One of the moons orbiting the Tresari Homeworld

HomeworldTresariMoon2.Description: One of the moons orbiting the Tresari Homeworld

HomeworldTresariMoon3.Description: One of the moons orbiting the Tresari Homeworld

HomeworldUCH.Description:
[c][e0e020]Note: [b]Dense atmosphere[/b][-][/c]
Reduced thruster performance and kinetic weapon range!

[c][b]Homeworld of the United Council of Humanity[/b]
(Andromeda Branch) [-][/c]

The first 'galactic colony' of Earth.

When this system's scout drone descended from orbit, it was still in power-save mode.

After touchdown, it reported back of a paradise world:
[i]Gentle climate, lush vegetation, huge ground deposits...[/i]

Yet once the Fleet arrived, [b]'New Terra'[/b] was not quite as expected... and there was no fuel left to search elsewhere.

HomeworldUCH_Moon1.Description: Luckily for the early settlers, there is plenty of water to mine here.

HomeworldUCH_Moon2.Description: This moon has a very thin atmosphere - It is shielded by an unusually large magetic field of the nearby planet, and the moon is dense enough to keep some gases from drifting into space. By this it has accumulated extreme amounts of desert varnish.

HomeworldUCH_Volcanic.Description:
[c][e0e020]WARNING:[-][/c]   [c][b]High gravity[/b]. Hostile.[-][/c]
\n[c][808080][i]Don't get stranded down there.[-][/i][-][/c] 
\n
\nA planet at the crossroads, shrouded in a thick cocktail of atmosphere:
\n
\nWill it become a boiling, or a frozen world? 
\n
\nOr something [i]just[-][/i] in between?
\n
\nWill all the gases stay up in the sky, or condensate, to rain down?
\n 
\nWill there be water? 
\n
\nAnd how long will it last, after all? 
\nWill it stay as oceans and lakes, or drift into space?
\n
\nEven if this planet grows too hostile for life... that won't discourage the visitors.
\n
\nThis is a young world. 
\nExpect rich ore deposits, a harsh climate, 
\nand laser shots from nowhere.

HomeworldZirax.Description: The Zirax Homeworld is a Sanctuary with only the spiritual and administrative personnel being allowed to land on

Hothouse.Description:
[c][808080][i]NOTE:   This playfield is a [b]WIP[/b].[-][/c][/i] 

[c][e0e020][b]Warning:[-][/c][/b]   [c]Crazy temperatures, hostile weather.[-][/c][/b] 
[c][e0e020][b]Warning:[-][/c][/b]   [c]Dense atmosphere![-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Ship thrust and ballistic weapon range reduced.[/i][-][/c]
[c][e0e020][b]Note:[-][/c][/b]   [c][b]Capital Vessel entry prohibited[/b] to prevent hull crush by atmospheric pressure[-][/c]

[c][i]- [u]Landfall is [b]not[/b] advised.[/u][/b][-][/i][-][/c]


... This is a [b]hothouse[/b].

Style and mystery from afar, - and brutally [i]dangerous[/i] up close!

Which is, what happens to a young, earth-like planet with a healthy magnetic field... circling just a [i]tiny[/i] bit too close to the fusion-powered fireball of a star. 
- Resulting in global warming that never looked back!

A thick blanket of a smog-like atmosphere, catching and reflecting each sun ray back to the ground.
Down there, it gets so hot that you could melt stones. 
Too hostile for water even to exist. - All of it has been boiled and reacted away. 

But others will take that up place:
Clouds of sulphuric acid, unload on ponds of molten lead composites. 
Weather cycles of an atmosphere that resembles moving in a 'less dense' swimming pool, all the while being crushed of the weight of a 'not-really'-ocean.

Yes, there are probably a lot of resources down there, ready to be claimed.
- As are the ships of those, who perished trying.

Whatever is the reason for a visit, 
there [i]must[/i] be better places than this ...!

Lava.Description:
Planets are born by the swirling, scattered ashes - the legacy of dead, exploded stars. 
...Gravity will collect all this messy debris, and give it a new form.

But a world's amount of rock and dust, slowly grinding, clumping together... will result in an enormous amount of friction.

Which means, [b][i]heat[/i][/b].

This place is just a mere quarter billion years old, and struggles to radiate all this excess heat into space -
The bigger the planet, the longer it takes.

In this liquid state, the planet resembles more a giant cooking pot, or lava lamp, or a blast furnace:
Continents of less dense slag skim the surface, while the heavy, exotic metals sink into the planet's deep inner.

Yet, by thermal churning and stirring, some bits of that precious core will be again swept back out, in reach of the surface.

For those who brave these temperatures, there will be great rewards.

LavaCollided.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]


[c][e0e020]Warning:[-][/c]   [c]High chance of [b]Patrol Vessel fleets[/b]![-][/c]
[i][c][808080][i][b]Please use caution, and keep your distance[/b][/i][-][/c]

This planet is the result of a collision of two worlds. 
The impact had to be really slow and at an shallow angle - - else there would be an asteroid belt orbiting the star!

This place is still so 'young', that there are no ore deposits around. 
But, in a way, it is still extremely [i][b]rich[/b][/i]...

- The cataclysm tore the old worlds so much into pieces, that all their core metals spilled everywhere. 
Even the shallow crust will have crystallized ores in small chunks, ready to be mined even with a hand drill (and a bit of ingenuity).
[i]But every larger spacefaring power out there, will try to claim such a place![/i]

If you choose to go meteorite hunting, please keep in mind:
Any larger boulder containing ores will not last long. With their higher density, they will vanish before your eyes - - quickly sink and dilute into the lava sea below."

LavaCollided2.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

[c][e0e020]Warning:[-][/c]   [c]High chance of [b]Patrol Vessel fleets[/b]![-][/c]
[i][c][808080][i][b]Please use caution, and keep your distance[/b][/i][-][/c]

This planet - and its nearby large moon - is the result of a collision of two worlds. 
The impact had to be really slow and at an shallow angle - - else there would be an asteroid belt orbiting the star!

This place is still so 'young', that there are no ore deposits around. 
But, in a way, it is still extremely [i][b]rich[/b][/i]...

- The cataclysm tore the old worlds so much into pieces, that all their core metals spilled everywhere. 
Even the shallow crust will have crystallized ores in small chunks, ready to be mined even with a hand drill (and a bit of ingenuity).
[i]But every larger spacefaring power out there, will try to claim such a place![/i]

If you choose to go meteorite hunting, please keep in mind:
Any larger boulder containing ores will not last long. With their higher density, they will vanish before your eyes - - quickly sink and dilute into the lava sea below."

LavaNascent.Description:
[c][e0e020]WARNING:[-][/c]   [c][b]High gravity[/b]. Hostile.[-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Don't get stranded down there.[-][/i][-][/c] 
A planet at the crossroads, shrouded in a thick cocktail of atmosphere:

Will it become a boiling, or a frozen world? 

Or something [i]just[-][/i] in between?

Will all the gases stay up in the sky, or condensate, to rain down?

Will there be water? 

And how long will it last, after all? 
Will it stay as oceans and lakes, or drift into space?

Even if this planet grows too hostile for life... that won't discourage the visitors.

This is a young world. 
Expect rich ore deposits, a harsh climate, 
and laser shots from nowhere.

LavaNascentDeepMine.Description:
[c][e0e020]WARNING:[-][/c]   [c][b]High gravity[/b]. Hostile.[-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Don't get stranded down there.[-][/i][-][/c] 
A planet at the crossroads, shrouded in a thick cocktail of atmosphere:

Will it become a boiling, or a frozen world? 

Or something [i]just[-][/i] in between?

Will all the gases stay up in the sky, or condensate, to rain down?

Will there be water? 

And how long will it last, after all? 
Will it stay as oceans and lakes, or drift into space?

Even if this planet grows too hostile for life... that won't discourage the visitors.

This is a young world. 
Expect rich ore deposits, a harsh climate, 
and laser shots from nowhere.

LavaOcean.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

Too young to even have a proper surface...

Moon.Description:
This barren rock carries its history on the surface: 
Completely solidified, and no atmosphere to remove tracks and shapes - 
All encounters will be remembered.

MoonAlienStarter.Description:
This world grows more teeth, with every heartbeat. 
Mutated creatures hunt each other in constant struggle, and uncontrolled military drones fire on shadows. 
NO tutorial support! 
Suitable for survivalists that want to battle their way to the stars.

MoonBarrenStarter.Description:
No oxygen. Few ores. - Just ruins, crumbling in the glaring day. 
And creatures waiting for you, to make their month. 
NO tutorial support! 
Suitable for advanced players.

MoonDesert.Description: This moon has a very thin atmosphere - It is shielded by an unusually large magetic field of the nearby planet, and the moon is dense enough to keep some gases from drifting into space. By this it has accumulated extreme amounts of desert varnish.

MoonDesertCanyon.Description:
[c]NOTE:   [b]POI detection limited[/b] on this moon.[/b] [-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Explore by sight.[-][/i][-][/c]
A moon with an extensive canyon system.

The water was 'imported' in the far past by a pentaxid-rich meteorite strike. - Either by natural means, or by an ancient attempt at terraforming. 
Now this world carries a thin atmosphere. - And with the passing of eons, even a few drops of rain will cut deep into the rock...

There is still low-density Pentaxid dust everywhere - - Masking the energy signatures of POIs.
[i]... A haven for anybody who does not want to be seen...[/i]

MoonForest.Description:
[c]Backwater world; meager resources. [-][/c]

A small temperate moon, with unusual thick atmosphere - Even bristling with life! 

[i]So many things[/i] need to come together for such a world to occur: Water, a still fluid metal core, a decent size, in the right distance to the star... 

But as a superficial scan indicates, this moon is [i]resource poor[/i]. So there is not much interest in things that empires do - To claim, exploit, settle, or even just to visit. 

The drama of the universe continues elsewhere. While the canopies of the trees reach for sunlight. 

It is still a great spot if you want to get out of sight, or just to stretch your legs and wander.

MoonIce.Description: An icy companion, with an ocean deep between its crust and rocky core. Faint organic substances leave colourful streaks on the surface. - A memory of a moon back in the Sol system...

MoonIce2.Description:
[c]Backwater world; meager resources. [-][/c]

[i][c][808080][i](Calculating the [b]map markers[/b] by statistical probability. - Sorry, no guarantees given!)[/i][-][/c]

[i]Once upon a time,[/i] ... the planets were still forming. Gaining mass and changing their paths around their star.

Gravitational disturbances swept through the whole system, hurtling planetesimals everywhere.

... This is a wanderer, born and expelled from the stellar asteroid belt. 
It got caught up by the nearby large planet - somehow avoiding collision or being flung away.

But during those narrow, chaotic first passes, it experienced massive tidal forces - 
Kneading it like dough, close to ripping it apart.


- Times are much calmer now.

And with that, all those scars make for impressive vistas and stories of adventure.

MoonIceDusted.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a WIP.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may still change with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

[c]Meager standard resources. - Junkyard.[-][/c]

This moon is beyond the planet's protective magnetosphere, but still close to its ring system.

By that, its leading side gathers huge amounts of dust... And possibly scoops up a lot of other things, that may circle a gas giant.

MoonLava.Description:
A baby moon, with a not yet solid core, and faint traces of an atmosphere. 
Maybe it was born out of a stellar disc, or it is the remnant of a spectacular planetary collision.

MoonLava2.Description: This infant moon already lost its atmosphere by the relentless push of the stellar wind. Undisturbed, the last open lava seas crystallize in bizarre forms.

MoonNascent.Description:
[c][e0e020]NOTE:[-][/c]   [c]Zirax resource survey in progress.[-][/c] 
A world in its youth: The atmosphere has cooled enough for it to rain. Last bits of the planetary neighbourhood crash in the surface, and violent jetstreams produce hail.
But in all this chaos, first life develops in the tide pools - filling the water with the colour of early photosynthesis.

MoonSnowStarter.Description:
A moon of former splendor.
Wedged between ice and starvation, both fauna and flora know what to do, to survive.
NO tutorial support!
Suitable for advanced players.

MoonStarter.Description:
This barren rock carries its history on the surface: 
Completely solidified, and no atmosphere to remove tracks and shapes -
All encounters will be remembered.

MoonStarterSystem.Description:
This barren rock carries its history on the surface: 
Completely solidified, and no atmosphere to remove tracks and shapes -
All encounters will be remembered.

MoonTemperateStarter.Description:
A charming, green paradise that will invite you in. 
But beware, even the most harmless creatures will know how to defend their turfs and treasures!

Recommended for Beginners and returning players. 
Tutorial support available!

NitrogenIce.Description:
[c][808080][i]NOTE:   This playfield is a [b]sketch[/b].[/i] 
 World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

[c]Meager standard resources. 
Low Gravity. Dark. [i][b]Cold[/b][/i].[-][/c]

A forsaken place without much strategic interest; where careless people get lost and stranded easily.

So far away from the host star. Nearly interstellar space.
And what is usally a gas, won’t stay as that for long. 
The place where everything comes to a rest.

... Frozen Nitrogen as a snow cover. Walking on air.
This is a strange winter world, taken to its extreme.

NitrogenVolcanic.Description:
[c][808080][i]NOTE:   This playfield is a [b]WIP[/b].[-][/c][/i] 

[c]Lonely. Dark. [i][b]Cold[/b][/i].
[c][808080]Meager resources - Buried deep, but very easy to mine.[-][/c]

Far away from the star, this world is so frozen that even Nitrogen stops being a gas, and condenses on its surface.

At such temperatures, fluid water takes the role of lava, while water ice is 'rock'.

Once a century, the star will heat this place barely enough for the water in the inner crust to expand - It then spews out to the surface, in a kind of colorful 'ice volcano', and distributes with it other materials all around.

This keeps the surface young; but also hides all meteorite impact craters (and their resources).


... Only very few people choose to live at such a harsh and remote place. 
[i]A tiny base for them to hide, to rest, to find calm, or maybe their own self...[/i]

Trying to ignore those military people, or those lawless ones, claiming [i]'this is the perfect spot!'[/i]

- They will come, they will go. The ice will stay.

Ocean.Description:
Travel serves more than war, trade, and leisure. 

Because the more isolated a place is - more the sooner than the later a humble life reaches that shore, settles down, and gets a chance to rock the old dominion:

Plant seeds as stowaways in the chinks of jackboots, the creatures of the ducts of container shuttles, or introduced as companions, or to hunt. 

- Whoever is unaccustomed to learn and change will be swept aside.

Omicron.Description:
[c][fbff00]Attention. This is a general broadcast call![-][/c]
All transportation to or from the planet Omicron without proper special permission is suspended until further notice. 

All personnel must register with the administration on Omicron Control Station!

OmicronMoon.Description:
[c][fbff00]Attention. This is a general broadcast call![-][/c]
All transportation to or from the planet Omicron without proper special permission is suspended until further notice.

All personnel must register with the administration on Omicron Control Station!

Onyx.Description: A planet-sized structure of unknown origin.

OnyxDestroyed.Description: A planet-sized structure. It has seen better days...

RadiatedIce.Description:
[c]NOTE:   This playfield is a sketch.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]  World may change considerably with future updates. Limited gameplay, yet.[-][/i][-][/c]

An ice world that slowly gets melted and sublimated away by the star's intense flares. 

... After all these millions of years, the regions that resist or reflect light better, will be left standing, as ''mountains''.

Scorched.Description:
[c][e0e020]Note:[-][/c]   [c]Deadly temperature differences![-][/c]
[c]Lots of resources, but [i][u]very[/u][/i] difficult to mine[-][/c]
[i][c][808080][i][b]Please use caution and prepare.[/b] 
You likely will need specialized equipment and vessels to thrive here![/i][-][/c]

This barren, rocky planet keeps nearest to its star... - Which is a bit too close for comfort.

Its day side is heated near to the melting point, while the night side radiates into the vacuum of space.
This constant temperature stress leaves deep scars in the surface, and fuels the growth of exotic crystals.

During youth, this world even had part of its crust boiled away - Placing the heavy mantle deposits in scanner range - 
... But you will need all your calm, skill and experience to collect them. - Lest it becomes a greedy miner's grave: Slowly being cooked in your own, stuck vessel!

This galaxy is huge. - - There are plenty of easier worlds to exploit, or home shores to settle.

People still attracted to such murderous places could be of the unstable, dangerous kind:
[i]The foolish, the violent, the crazy, the lawless...[/i]

Ducking away, underground or in the shadows - Escaping the glare of the day... while their bases crumble and groan by the stretching of metal.

Snow.Description:
Water is the great equalizer.

Is it too hot? Then it will soak up the energy, to keep it for later. - Still liquid!
Or too cold? - The water will move and spread the warmth around.

And its ice will swim, and insulate the ocean below from freezing solid.
By this, even a world of all-encompassing snow might change, evolve and thrive.

Dare to exist near to a fickle, unpredictable star? 
Or far away, at the outer edges of its generosity?
... Between hot and cold, with [i]water[/i] there is always a place for life!

Snow2.Description:
A world at the far edge of the habitable zone, deep in the throes of a major ice age. Watch out for hot tubs and warm areas around lakes and in lower altitudes. The atmosphere is breathable.
A challenging start, intended for [c][FFFFFF]experienced survivalists[-][/c] with a passion for hot beverages.

[i]- Instant freezing, use the heater. Night kills! 
- High risk of starvation; sparse resources.[/i]

SnowStarter.Description:
A world at the far edge of the habitable zone, deep in the throes of a major ice age. Watch out for hot tubs and warm areas around lakes and in lower altitudes. The atmosphere is breathable.
A challenging start, intended for [c][FFFFFF]experienced survivalists[-][/c] with a passion for hot beverages.

[i]- Instant freezing, use the heater. Night kills! 
- High risk of starvation; sparse resources.[/i]

SnowStory.Description:
Water is the great equalizer.

Is it too hot? Then it will soak up the energy, to keep it for later. - Still liquid!
Or too cold? - The water will move and spread the warmth around.

And its ice will swim, and insulate the ocean below from freezing solid.
By this, even a world of all-encompassing snow might change, evolve and thrive.

Dare to exist near to a fickle, unpredictable star? 
Or far away, at the outer edges of its generosity?
... Between hot and cold, with [i]water[/i] there is always a place for life!

StoryArid.Description:
Parched mountains, rugged savanna and grueling plains, shriveled lake-beds with the remains of once thriving plant and animal life. This hot, dry and unrelenting planet has a thin atmosphere that is un-breathable by humans. A [c][FFFFFF]more challenging[-][/c] start for players that like to face the dangers of heat and the lack of oxygen.

[i]- Cool down with water; generate oxygen with the condenser.
- Hostile weather and aggressive neighbours will arrive. Be prepared.[/i]

StoryPrairie.Description:
An older planet. Bright sun and dry land.
With Plants and Fungi.
[i]One[/i] of them invaded this world.
But after all this time, who can still tell...?

The extremes died off, competition gave way to truce- And then to mutual exchange of nutrients.
Until, suddenly, one life depends on the other.

StorySnow.Description:
Water is the great equalizer.

Is it too hot? Then it will soak up the energy, to keep it for later. - Still liquid!
Or too cold? - The water will move and spread the warmth around.

And its ice will swim, and insulate the ocean below from freezing solid.
By this, even a world of all-encompassing snow might change, evolve and thrive.

Dare to exist near to a fickle, unpredictable star? 
Or far away, at the outer edges of its generosity?
... Between hot and cold, with [i]water[/i] there is always a place for life!

Story_Wuastel.Description:
A world in deep in blacklight.
(Wear lots of sunscreen.)

The atmosphere contains gases that are only passable for ultraviolet rays.
While those are invisible to the human eye, from surfaces they can still be reflected back as 'light' - Resulting in bleak-yet-colourful landscapes.

TemperatePlateau.Description:
Ancient and rare, this world is proof of life's resilience.

Some eons ago, a stellar merger resulted in a hyperflare—stripping an ocean planet of its water.

Now, former islands are towering high in the sky, with a mix of dense woodland and open glades far below it.

Such a landscape creates a unique three-step weather pattern:
Snow falls on the plateaus, and on sunny days, sublimates directly back into the atmosphere—creating a kind of extreme, planet-wide 'morning fog'.
This water then deposits as ice in the shadows of the middle altitudes, where it slowly melts, feeding the warm bottom ponds.

TemperatePlateauStory.Description:
Difficulty: [c][FFFFFF] Easy[-][/c]    Origin: [c][FFFFFF] %Origin%[-][/c]

An unusual planet with vast plateaus towering high in the sky, and a mix of vast woodland and open glades. The climate is moderate and it has a breathable atmosphere. Resources are plentiful though the terrain could prove challenging.

Start: [c][FFFFFF]Escape Pod\\[-][/c]

TemperatePrairie.Description:
An older planet. Bright sun and dry land.
With Plants and Fungi.
[i]One[/i] of them invaded this world.
But after all this time, who can still tell...?

The extremes died off, competition gave way to truce- And then to mutual exchange of nutrients.
Until, suddenly, one life depends on the other.

TemperateStarter.Description:
Lakes, swamps, deep forests, oceans and vast open plains are the geographical characteristics of this temperate planet. It has a multitude of mountain ranges with snow-capped peaks, some formed by volcanic activity, others by glacial flow. The climate is moderate and the atmosphere breathable.

[i]- If this is your first stay, please consider [c][FFFFFF]playing the tutorial first[-][/c] (select in [c][FFFFFF]main menu[-][/c]).
- Avoid unprepared food. Creatures can make you sick.[/i]

TemperateStarter2.Description:
Start: [c][FFFFFF]Wreckage Base[-][/c]        Equipment:[c][FFFFFF] Advanced[-][/c]        Tutorial:[c][FFFFFF] No[-][/c]

Lakes, swamps, deep forests, oceans and vast open plains are the geographical characteristics of this temperate planet. With a multitude of mountain ranges with snow-capped peaks, some formed by volcanic activity, others by glacial flow, the climate is moderate and the atmosphere is breathable.

Recommended for experienced players who do not need the basic tutorial and want to start in a base with advanced equipment.

TemperateSwamp.Description:
A life-bearing world. Protected from radiation by a thick atmosphere - which also keeps the heat in.
Rich in water but poor in soils, which creates a fertile breeding ground for dangerous creatures.

TemperateSwampStarter.Description:
A swamp planet, which is a fertile breeding ground for dangerous creatures. At least it has a breathable atmosphere. This world is a relief for [c][FFFFFF]advanced players[-][/c] and their guns. To all others: do not go into the swamp at night!

[i]- Plentiful in resources, but much to fight here.
- A Patrol Vessel might hunt you down.
